channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: MIT rejects Trump admin funding compact, citing free expression concerns
item.description: The Massachusetts Institute of Technology (MIT) rejected a Trump administration proposal that would usher in sweeping reforms in exchange for benefits.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A sacred lake, a Columbus statue, and an Indigenous people's long struggle for land
item.description: GEDDES, N.Y. (AP) — Before white settlers came to Onondaga Lake, before the city of Syracuse grew along its shore, before the pristine waters became one of the most polluted bodies of water in the United States, it was a sacred place for the Onondaga Nation.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL HILL, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Two powerful quakes strike off southern Philippines, killing at least 7 people
item.description: MANILA, Philippines (AP) — Two powerful offshore earthquakes struck the same region in the southern Philippines hours apart on Friday with the first 7.4 magnitude temblor killing at least seven people, setting off landslides and prompting evacuations of coastal areas nearby because of a brief tsunam
item.dc:creator: JIM GOMEZ, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: A look at how Venezuelans in the US are reacting to Maria Corina Machado's Nobel Prize win
item.description: DORAL, Fla. (AP) — Venezuelans in “Little Venezuela” — the largest home for the country's natives in the United States — are welcoming the news that opposition leader Maria Corina Machado won the Nobel Peace Prize with bittersweetness as deportation threats loom.
item.dc:creator: GISELA SALOMON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: New Orleans is electing a new mayor and a wide field is pledging change after indictments
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — New Orleans voters on Saturday are picking a new mayor to replace two-term incumbent LaToya Cantrell, whose indictment on corruption charges has left a wide field of candidates promising change to city hall.
item.dc:creator: JACK BROOK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Thousands of Palestinians return to what's left of their homes as Gaza ceasefire takes effect
item.description: WADI GAZA, Gaza Strip (AP) — Tens of thousands of Palestinians headed back to the heavily destroyed northern Gaza Strip on Friday as a U.S.-brokered ceasefire came into effect in a deal that raised hopes for ending the Israel-Hamas war. All the remaining hostages were set to be released within days.
item.dc:creator: ABDEL KAREEM HANA, WAFAA SHURAFA and MELANIE LIDMAN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: OSHA violations reported prior to massive explosion at Tennessee facility: documents
item.description: As investigators continue to comb through the wreckage of an explosion in Tennessee that left 18 people unaccounted for Friday, Nexstar's WKRN has learned more about the company that operated the facility.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Wilson, KRON4

item.title: Bodycam video: Sisters arrested for attacking gate agent at airport
item.description: Newly-released body camera video shows the moment two sisters were arrested for allegedly assaulting a Frontier Airlines gate agent.
item.dc:creator: Sara Kitchin, NewsNation

item.title: Erika Kirk addresses grief in heartfelt social media post one month after husband's assassination
item.description: Charlie Kirk's widow, Erika Kirk, reflected on the grieving process in a social media post, describing the non-linear nature of loss after his Sept. 10 assassination at Utah Valley University.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: AstraZeneca agrees to lower drug prices for Medicaid under Trump administration deal
item.description: AstraZeneca on Friday became the second major pharmaceutical manufacturer to announce it had agreed to lower the cost of prescription drugs for Medicaid under a deal struck with the Trump administration that avoided its threats of steep tariffs.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Nor'easter without a name to bring ocean flooding and high winds to US East Coast
item.description: A storm without a name was expected to bring flooding to the East Coast from South Carolina to New Jersey and high winds to New York City over the holiday weekend.
item.dc:creator: JEFFREY COLLINS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: 22 states are in a recession or on the brink: Moody’s
item.description: It’s more sobering news for the economy as last week U.S. hiring plans fell to their lowest level since 2009.
item.dc:creator: Alicia Nieves, Fox 5

item.title: WGN employee detained amid ICE activity in Chicago, later released
item.description: An employee of WGN-TV was detained by two Border Patrol officers and later released after federal agents swarmed a busy intersection in Chicago Friday Morning.
item.dc:creator: Christine Flores, WFLA, NBC

item.title: National Guard stands down in Illinois while troops patrol in Memphis with local police
item.description: MEMPHIS, Tenn. (AP) — National Guard troops patrolled in Tennessee's second-largest city for the first time Friday while soldiers in Illinois were engaged only in planning and training after a judge handed a loss to the Trump administration and blocked their deployment to the Chicago area.
item.dc:creator: ADRIAN SAINZ and CHRISTINE FERNANDO, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump threatens tech export limits, new 100% tariff on Chinese imports starting Nov. 1 or sooner
item.description: Trump said that “starting November 1st, 2025 (or sooner, depending on any further actions or changes taken by China), the United States of America will impose a Tariff of 100% on China, over and above any Tariff that they are currently paying.
item.dc:creator: KRON4

item.title: AstraZeneca agrees to lower drug prices for Medicaid under Trump administration deal
item.description: AstraZeneca on Friday became the second major pharmaceutical manufacturer to announce it had agreed to lower the cost of prescription drugs for Medicaid under a deal struck with the Trump administration that avoided its threats of steep tariffs.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Sister Jean Dolores Schmidt, beloved chaplain at Loyola University Chicago, dies at 106
item.description: Sister Jean Dolores Schmidt, a beloved member of the Loyola University Chicago community who was well-known as the chaplain to the Loyola University men's basketball team, died Thursday at the age of 106, according to the university.
item.dc:creator: Ethan Illers, Fox 5

item.title: Some Palestinians pack up and move north toward their homes in Gaza after ceasefire goes into effect
item.description: WADI GAZA, Gaza Strip (AP) — Tens of thousands of Palestinians displaced by the war in Gaza started walking north Friday toward homes they were forced to flee — or what remains of them — after the Israeli military announced a ceasefire between Israel and Hamas had gone into effect.
item.dc:creator: ABDEL KAREEM HANA and WAFAA SHURAFA, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Man sentenced for releasing noxious gas cloud at Meijer
item.description: A man will spend years in prison after a cloud of noxious gas was released during a theft at a grocery store in Michigan.
item.dc:creator: Katie Rosendale, NewsNation

item.title: DHS blames Democrats for shutdown in video to be shown at TSA checkpoints
item.description: The Department of Homeland Security (DHS) is rolling out a new video of DHS Secretary Kristi Noem blaming Democrats for the government shutdown,
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, WFLA, NBC

item.title: GOP senators urge RFK Jr. to crack down on medication abortion
item.description: Almost every Senate Republican on Thursday urged the Food and Drug Administration (FDA) to “reevaluate” whether a newly approved generic version of the abortion drug mifepristone should stay on the market. The letter to Health and Human Services (HHS) Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, NewsNation

item.title: GOP senators worry Trump is undercutting their shutdown strategy
item.description: Senate Republicans are grumbling that President Donald Trump undercut their strategy for ending the government shutdown when he said that he’s willing to cut “the right deal” with Democrats on health care.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, KRON4

item.title: Arizona sheriff's office misused millions meant to remedy racial profiling, report reveals
item.description: PHOENIX (AP) — The sheriff’s office for metro Phoenix spent millions of dollars budgeted for compliance costs in a racial profiling case over Joe Arpaio’s immigration crackdowns on things that had little or nothing to do with a court-ordered overhaul of the agency, according to an expert’s report.
item.dc:creator: JACQUES BILLEAUD, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: After a police pursuit of a huge inflatable pumpkin, body cam video shows officer struggling with it
item.description: PARMA HEIGHTS, Ohio (AP) — The subject of an Ohio police pursuit took off down the road, a flash of orange in the darkness, but officers eventually caught up and grabbed the escapee: a runaway inflatable pumpkin as tall as them.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Israel, Hamas agree to 'first phase' of plan to end fighting, release hostages, Trump says
item.description: Israel and Hamas have agreed to the “first phase” of his peace plan to pause fighting and release at least some hostages and prisoners, U.S. President Donald Trump said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Chicago elementary school students meet Pope Leo XIV in Vatican City
item.description: VATICAN CITY — Students from Our Lady of Mount Carmel Academy in Lakeview met Pope Leo XIV in Vatican City on Wednesday. The children attended the pontiff's weekly audience — some dressed as the pope, as the swiss guard and cardinals.
item.dc:creator: Marisa Rodriguez, ABC 4

item.title: Staffing shortages cause more US flight delays as government shutdown reaches 7th day
item.description: Staffing shortages led to more flight delays at airports across the U.S. on Tuesday as the federal government shutdown stretched into a seventh day, while union leaders for air traffic controllers and airport security screeners warned the situation was likely to get worse.
item.dc:creator: RIO YAMAT and JOSH FUNK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Disneyland guest dies after riding Haunted Mansion
item.description: A woman in her 60s suffered an apparent heart attack and died after riding the Haunted Mansion attraction at Disneyland on Monday evening.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, ABC 4

item.title: Changes to SNAP benefits take effect in October: What to know
item.description: The federal government may be shut down, but that didn't stop changes to SNAP benefits from kicking in on the first of the month, which is also the start of the fiscal year.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Arrivals of international students to the US dropped almost a fifth in August, data shows
item.description: The number of international student arrivals in the U.S. dropped by nearly a fifth in August as American universities started the new academic year, according to federal data.
item.dc:creator: MAKIYA SEMINERA and CHRISTOPHER L. KELLER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Man charged with having explosive materials near DC church hosting annual Red Mass celebration
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A New Jersey man has been arrested on charges that he had explosive materials in his possession near a Washington, D.C., church that was preparing to celebrate an annual Mass marking the start of the U.S. Supreme Court's term, according to police and court records.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL KUNZELMAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Bob Ross paintings to be auctioned to support public TV stations after federal funding cuts
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Thirty paintings created by the bushy-haired, soft-spoken Bob Ross will soon be up for auction to defray the costs of programming for public television stations suffering from cuts in federal funding.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: White House says it will use tariff money to fund nutrition program amid shutdown
item.description: White House press secretary Karoline Leavitt posted on X that President Trump and the White House had identified the "creative solution" to shift tariff funds to the Special Supplemental Nutrition Program for Women, Infants and Children, commonly known as WIC, which was set to run out of money in th
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5

item.title: Truck driver sues Mark Sanchez and Fox after violent fight over parking space
item.description: A 69-year-old truck driver who was seriously injured in a fight that prosecutors say was started by former NFL quarterback and sports analyst Mark Sanchez is suing Sanchez and his employer, Fox Corporation, in Indiana state court.
item.dc:creator: MARGERY A. BECK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Senate rejects dueling government funding bills on Day 6 of shutdown
item.description: The Senate on Monday pushed the government shutdown to the one-week mark as Democrats blocked the GOP's "clean" stopgap funding bill from advancing for a fifth time.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, WGN 9

item.title: South Carolina's top law official says no arson evidence so far in fire that destroyed judge's home
item.description: COLUMBIA, S.C. (AP) — State agents have so far found no evidence that a fire that destroyed a judge's home on a South Carolina island was intentionally set, the state's top law enforcement official said Monday.
item.dc:creator: JEFFREY COLLINS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: National Guard troops report to Illinois as lawsuit against Trump administration seeks to halt their deployment
item.description: CHICAGO (AP/WGN) — Hundreds of Illinois National Guard troops reported for duty in the Chicago area Tuesday morning, and they'll be joined by members of the Texas National Guard for what the Trump administration is calling a "federal protective mission.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9
